edibility: Not foraged for human consumption; only infects ants (insect-pathogenic). Some related Ophiocordyceps species are used as traditional Chinese medicine tonics for energy and vitality.
taste: Not eaten by humans; faintly mushroomy if tasted in TCM preparation.
search: Found in tropical rainforests on the underside of leaves at the precise leaf-vein height where infected ants died. A long club-like fruiting body sprouts from the ant's head after death. Not a foraging target for humans.

toxicity.symptoms: Lethal to ants; harmless to humans (host specificity)
